title: lotus corniculatus var. arvensis
intro: Lotus corniculatus var. arvensis, also known as the common bird's-foot trefoil, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive bird-like shape of its seed pods. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of consuming lotus corniculatus var. arvensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Lotus corniculatus var. arvensis is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
